content.problemStatement:
A teacher wants to distribute chocolate packets to MMM students. There are NNN packets available, and the ithi^\text{th}ith packet contains AiA_iAi​ chocolates. The teacher wants to be fair and select MMM consecutive packets to distribute such that the difference between the maximum number of chocolates in a packet given to a student and the minimum number of chocolates in a packet given to a student is as small as possible. Each student receives exactly one packet. Find this minimum possible difference.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN, the number of packets.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space separated integers, AiA_iAi​, representing the number of chocolates in each packet.
The third line contains an integer MMM, the number of students.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single integer representing the minimum possible difference.

code.Python3:
t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    n = int(input())
    arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
    m = int(input())

    if m == 0 or n == 0 or m > n:
        print(0)
        continue

    arr.sort()
    min_diff = float('inf')

    for i in range(n - m + 1):
        diff = arr[i + m - 1] - arr[i]
        if diff < min_diff:
            min_diff = diff

    print(min_diff)
